subject: Sociology
title: Who Gets Into University?
description: A 60-second lesson on Who Gets Into University?.

hook.text: A poor student with perfect grades and a rich student with average grades apply. Guess who gets in.

buildup.text: University access has expanded dramatically, but elite institutions remain dominated by wealthy families.

discovery.text: Legacy admissions, test prep, and private counselors give affluent applicants invisible advantages.

twist.text: Even when poor students get accepted, financial barriers, culture shock, and isolation drive many to drop out.

climax.text: University access expanded but elite access didn't. Mass education coexists with persistent class exclusion.

punchline.text: The doors opened but not equally.

quiz.question: What advantages do wealthy students have in university admissions?
options: Legacy admissions, test prep, and private counselors
